stories.title: First groups designated as threat to national security
stories.summary: The Home Office has formally designated the IRGC, IMCR, and the GRU Volunteer Corps as threats to national security under new state threats powers. This marks the first use of new designation powers introduced to protect the UK from hostile state activity.

stories.title: 'No secrecy' over asylum camp decision, court told
stories.summary: A court heard arguments that there was 'no secrecy' over the Home Office's decision regarding asylum seeker camp plans. The case centres on whether the government's asylum camp decision was lawful.

stories.title: Ministerial Appointments: July 2026
stories.summary: Rt Hon Shabana Mahmood MP confirmed to remain as Secretary of State for the Home Department following ministerial reshuffle. Full list of ministerial appointments published on GOV.UK.

stories.title: Public Sector Fraud Authority to transfer to DWP
stories.summary: As part of Machinery of Government changes, the Public Sector Fraud Authority will transfer to the Department for Work and Pensions, with continued sponsorship from HM Treasury. This restructuring aims to consolidate fraud prevention efforts.

stories.title: £708 million to accelerate UK next-generation air combat technology
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence announced £708 million in funding to accelerate development of next-generation air combat technology for the UK. The investment supports the UK's future combat air programme.

stories.title: MOD awards £20 million contract to develop hypersonic capability
stories.summary: The Ministry of Defence has awarded a £20 million contract to develop hypersonic weapons capability for the UK armed forces. This is part of the UK's broader defence modernisation agenda.

stories.title: New Defence Secretary Wes Streeting promises MOD modernisation
stories.summary: In his first speech as Defence Secretary, Wes Streeting pledged to modernise the Ministry of Defence, embrace AI and cut waste. He stated that a £15bn increase in military spending announced by the last government is not yet fully funded.

stories.title: DBT to be renamed Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade (DBIST)
stories.summary: As part of major Machinery of Government changes, the Department for Business and Trade (DBT) will be renamed to the Department for Business, Innovation, Science and Trade (DBIST). This reflects a broader restructuring of government science and technology functions.

agencies.name: DSIT
agencies.description: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

stories.title: DSIT functions to be redistributed in government restructure
stories.summary: The functions of the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) will be redistributed to align with the Government's core economic and social priorities as part of Machinery of Government changes. Science and innovation functions are being reorganised across departments.

stories.title: Thousands with MS to get 'life-changing' drug fampridine on NHS
stories.summary: NHS England announced that fampridine — the first medicine designed to improve walking in adults with multiple sclerosis — will now be routinely available on the NHS. Thousands of MS patients are set to benefit from this 'life-changing' treatment.

stories.title: Jet pioneer Sir Frank Whittle celebrated with new Royal Mail stamps
stories.summary: Royal Mail has issued a new set of special stamps celebrating Sir Frank Whittle, the British engineer who pioneered the jet engine. The commemorative stamps mark his landmark contribution to aviation history.
